content.problemStatement:
Dr.D is now fond of matrices. He is particularly interested in Doofish Matrices. A Doofish Matrix of order NNN is a matrix with NNN rows (numbered 111 through NNN) and NNN columns (numbered 111 through NNN) such that:


Each element of this matrix is an integer between 111 and 2Nâˆ’12N-12Nâˆ’1 inclusive.
For each iii and jjj (1â‰¤iâ‰¤N1 \le i \le N1â‰¤iâ‰¤N, 1â‰¤jâ‰¤2Nâˆ’11 \le j \le 2N-11â‰¤jâ‰¤2Nâˆ’1), the integer jjj appears in the iii-th row and/or in the iii-th column.
Now, for a given NNN, Dr.D is wondering if there is a Doofish Matrix of order NNN. Find such a matrix or determine that it does not exist. If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one.
constraints: the sum of N2N^2N2 over all test cases does not exceed 4â‹…1064 \cdot 10^64â‹…106
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (29 points):** NNN is a power of 222
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


First, print a line containing the string `"Hooray"` if a Doofish Matrix of order NNN exists or `"Boo"` if it does not.
If it exists, print NNN more lines describing one such matrix. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines should contain NNN space-separated integers â€• the elements in the iii-th row.
